Class Function

java.lang.Object
org.key_project.logic.op.AbstractOperator
org.key_project.logic.op.AbstractSortedOperator
org.key_project.logic.op.Function
All Implemented Interfaces:
Named, Operator, SortedOperator, Sorted, SyntaxElement
Direct Known Subclasses:
JFunction
public abstract class Function extends AbstractSortedOperator
Objects of this class represent function and predicate symbols. Note that program variables are a separate syntactic category, and not a type of function.

  • Constructor Details

    • Function

      protected Function(Name name, ImmutableArray<Sort> argSorts, Sort sort, ImmutableArray<Boolean> whereToBind, boolean isRigid, boolean unique, boolean isSkolemConstant)

  • Method Details

    • isUnique

      public final boolean isUnique()
      Indicates whether the function or predicate symbol has the "uniqueness" property. For two unique symbols f1: A1 -> B1, f2: A2 -> B2 by definition we have (1) f1(x) != f1(y) for all x, y in A1 with x != y (i.e., injectivity), and (2) f1(x) != f2(y) for all x in A1, y in A2.

    • isSkolemConstant

      public final boolean isSkolemConstant()

      public final String toString()

      toString in class AbstractOperator

    • getChild

      public SyntaxElement getChild(int n)
      Description copied from interface: SyntaxElement
      Get the n-th child of this syntax element.
      Parameters:
      n - index of the child.
      Returns:
      the n-th child of this syntax element.
